game_name: Indiana Jones and the Temple of Doom

design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate temple levels as Indiana Jones, avoiding hazards and enemies while progressing toward confrontation with Mola Ram
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Platforming precision against environmental and enemy threats in exotic temple settings
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Embodying Indiana Jones exploring a dangerous temple, solving its dangers
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Adapted arcade game with significant gameplay departures including added ending, cut levels, and redesigned level layouts specific to NES capabilities

difficulty.lives_system: Multiple lives with loss on contact with hazards/enemies

progression.unlock_mechanism: Level completion gates access to subsequent temple areas
replayability_hooks: NES arcade-style pattern memorization encourages replay mastery
Multiple level variations between source arcade and NES version creates distinct experience

world_building_techniques: Visual temple theming and enemy variety establish Indiana Jones adventure setting
Film IP provides external narrative context; game focuses on mechanical challenge

boss_design.philosophy: Climactic confrontation with Mola Ram as intended endpoint, contrasting with original arcade's continuing cycle

danger_signals: Temple environment with hostile guards and traps signals danger
Enemy variety (cultists, animals) establishes hostile territory
environmental_storytelling: Temple architecture and aesthetic establishes exotic adventure setting
Level theming communicates progression through different temple sections

sources_used: tcrf - Indiana Jones and the Temple of Doom (NES) article
